name: Coinstar Kiosk
description_short: Coinstar operates self-service kiosks offering coin-to-cash conversion, cryptocurrency purchases, Amazon Cash loading, and account transfers at retail locations nationwide.
description_long:
Coinstar is a long-established operator of self-service kiosks found in grocery stores and retail locations across the United States. The company was recently acquired by Arctic Slope Regional Corporation (ASRC) as of February 2026. Coinstar kiosks provide multiple financial services beyond traditional coin exchange, positioning themselves as convenient alternatives to bank visits for various cash and digital currency needs.

The company's core offering remains Coins to Cash®, which converts loose change into paper currency without requiring customers to sort, count, or roll coins. Beyond coin services, Coinstar has significantly expanded into cryptocurrency services through partnerships with Coinme and their proprietary CINQ by Coinstar platform, allowing customers to convert cash into 25+ cryptocurrency forms. Additional services include Amazon Cash (loading funds to Amazon accounts with $5-$500 limits), eGift card exchanges with no-fee options, charitable donations through their Coins that Count® program, and account transfers to checking accounts via partner financial institutions. The kiosks also issue CINQ debit cards.

Coinstar distinguishes itself through its ubiquitous retail presence and multi-service approach that combines traditional financial services with emerging fintech offerings. The company explicitly states that kiosks do NOT exchange gift cards or foreign currency, and accepts bills only at select locations (this specific Long Island City kiosk does not accept bills). Their expansion into cryptocurrency reflects an attempt to serve customers interested in digital assets through familiar physical infrastructure.

While Coinstar provides genuine convenience for coin conversion and emerging financial services, consumers should note that fees vary by location and service type. The cryptocurrency offerings require downloading separate apps (Coinme or CINQ) prior to visiting a kiosk. The company's breadth of services may obscure that not all kiosks offer all services, and some services like account transfers charge fees that vary by financial institution.
pros: Converts coins to cash immediately without requiring customers to count, sort, or roll coins
Located at grocery stores and retail locations for convenient access without visiting a bank
eGift card exchange option with NO FEE options available for certain brands
Amazon Cash service allows loading $5-$500 directly to Amazon account balance with no hidden fees
Cryptocurrency services (Coinme, CINQ) enable cash-to-crypto conversion at physical kiosks
Charity donation option (Coins that Count®) allows direct donations to participating nonprofits
Account transfer service lets customers deposit coins/cash directly to checking accounts
cons: Coin-to-cash conversion fees vary by location and are not transparent on the website
Does not exchange gift cards or foreign currency despite general financial services positioning
Not all kiosks accept bills (this specific location does not), limiting cash service universality
Account transfers and some other services charge variable fees depending on financial institution partner
Cryptocurrency services require downloading separate third-party apps before visiting kiosk
best_for: Consumers with loose change who want immediate cash conversion without bank visits
Amazon shoppers wanting to add funds to their account using cash at retail locations
Cryptocurrency-curious customers seeking accessible entry points through familiar retail locations
Charity donors who want to convert spare change into nonprofit donations
diagnosis: Coinstar kiosks are best for consumers seeking convenient coin-to-cash conversion at retail locations without bank visits, and for those interested in cryptocurrency or Amazon purchases through physical kiosk infrastructure. The primary caveat is that fees vary significantly by location and service type, and not all kiosks offer all services—customers should verify specific kiosk capabilities before visiting.
services: eGift card exchange (multiple retailers, some fee-free)
CINQ by Coinstar cryptocurrency conversion (25+ crypto options)
Charity donations via Coins that Count® program
CINQ by Coinstar debit card issuance
